definition_population.content: Behçet disease (BD) is an inflammatory multisystem disorder of unknown etiology, triggered by infection and environmental factors in genetically predisposed individuals; presents with recurrent oral/genital ulcers, skin lesions, and uveitis (ICD-10 M35.2).

sources.quote: Behçet disease (BD) is an inflammatory multisystem disorder of unknown etiology, believed to be triggered by infection and environmental factors in genetically predisposed individuals.

epidemiology.content: Global prevalence ~10.3 per 100,000, higher along the historic Silk Route (Turkey, Middle East, East Asia).

sources.quote: prevalence estimated at 10.3 (95% CI, 6.1, 17.7) per 100,000 population, with higher rates observed in regions historically linked to the Silk Route.

biology.content: GWAS identified NPHP4 as a novel genome-wide-significant susceptibility locus for overall Behçet disease (replicated across Korean, Turkish, Japanese cohorts). Broader immunopathogenesis involves disrupted Th1/Th2 and Th17/Treg balance and B-cell activation.

sources.quote: We identified three novel genome-wide significant susceptibility loci including NPHP4
sources.note: P-value exponents after '10' were rendered blank/truncated in the source abstract as returned by PubMed; omitted rather than guessed.

sources.quote: increased IL-17, IL-10, T-bet/GATA-3, and retinoic acid related orphan receptor (ROR)-γt/Foxp3 ratios, marking the disrupted scale of the Th1/Th2 and Th17/Treg axis

treatment.content: TNF-alpha inhibitors are pivotal biologics; IL-1 inhibitors and ustekinumab broaden options. Apremilast (Otezla, FDA NDA205437, approved 2014-03-21) is effective for BD oral ulcers.

sources.quote: treatment strategies, highlighting TNF-alpha inhibitors as pivotal biologics and newer agents like IL-1 inhibitors and Ustekinumab that broaden the therapeutic options for BD.
current evidence shows that apremilast is effective for oral ulcer, the most common symptom of Behçet's disease.

regulatory_trials.content: 70 interventional trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov. Notable ongoing Phase 2 trial NCT06849908 (baricitinib vs adalimumab, intestinal BD, recruiting).

sources.quote: A Multi-center, Prospective, Open-label, Randomized Study to Explore Efficacy and Safety of Baricitinib in Refractory Intestinal Behçet's Syndrome Patients

gaps: No direct mechanistic study found linking NPHP4 function to CD4+ T-cell biology specifically; the GWAS association is genetic, not functional.
A fuller deep-dive dossier for a related autoimmune indication (rheumatoid arthritis) already exists in this project and was not re-researched here.
